author: .paulharris
content:
https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/585
we've got several of these now where events seem to need changes...
I think we probably need to agree on how to address it as a concept.

We'd previously discussed adding new events to differentiate versions

Another option would be to add a version tag to these objects as an optional field, but this would mean we need to verify clients don't die if theres an extra field that becomes visible... seems like the best way forward but interested in peoples opinions.

author: nflaig
content:
at least for new events that emit data based on spec containers it makes sense to have a version tag, ie. wrap them into `{"version": "fork_name", "data": { ... }}`, should apply this to [these events](https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/blob/master/apis/eventstream/index.yaml#L163-L172)

we do not need to support ssz so clients shouldn't blow up if new fields are added or removed between forks, so [#585](https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/585) seems fine to me but it would be great if we can come up with a better way on how to define the event format/schema in the spec, doing it based on example values which can only ever have a single value is not great

author: .paulharris
content: Ah we could probably have them well defined at each fork in the spec - some do get consumed by validators etc...
